acceptanceSignals: Single app serves `/employee/*`, `/vendor/*`, `/admin/*` with role-aware nav.

lastCommit.message: feat: implement Shared Frontend Platform and UX Baseline (attempt 3)

attempts.latestEventSummary: feature completed: Shared Frontend Platform and UX Baseline in 0 attempts

reinforcementResults.name: Contract Lockstep Gate
reinforcementResults.summary: No blocking issues found. The workspace meets the feature baseline: role-aware shared routing for `/employee/*`, `/vendor/*`, `/admin/*`, centralized `zh-TW` UI copy, and global loading/offline/localized-error UX are present in the web platform layer. Policy is satisfied for this feature because the feature diff is frontend-only (no backend/OpenAPI/generated-client contract boundary changes), so no contract-sync update was required in this scope. Residual risk: CI pass state is not directly observable from workspace files.

reinforcementResults.name: Authorization Boundary Gate
reinforcementResults.summary: Pass. The feature responsibilities and active policy checks are satisfied in the current workspace: `/employee/*`, `/vendor/*`, `/admin/*` routing and role-aware nav are centralized (`apps/web/src/hooks.server.ts`, `apps/web/src/lib/platform/navigation.ts`, `apps/web/src/routes/+layout.svelte`); zh-TW UI copy is centralized (`apps/web/src/lib/i18n/zh-tw.ts`); global loading/offline/localized-error UX exists (`apps/web/src/routes/+layout.svelte`, `apps/web/src/routes/+error.svelte`); and frontend API access is centralized through the generated TS client only (`apps/web/src/lib/platform/api/client.ts`). Auth boundary evidence is present via tests for route isolation (`apps/web/tests/hooks.test.ts`), token refresh plus rejection paths (`apps/web/tests/runtime.test.ts`), and role/scope denial in privileged HTTP/API authorization flows (`tests/identity_access_governance.rs`, `src/access.rs`, `src/transport/http.rs`). Test execution in this environment is blocked by sandbox EPERM (tsx IPC socket), so this verdict is based on static review plus test coverage presence.

reinforcementResults.name: UX Reliability Gate
reinforcementResults.summary: Workspace review indicates this feature currently satisfies the active policy. Evidence shows a single SvelteKit app serving `/employee/*`, `/vendor/*`, and `/admin/*` with role-aware navigation/guards (`navigation.ts`, `guards.ts`, `hooks.server.ts`); zh-TW copy centralized in `src/lib/i18n/zh-tw.ts` and consumed across shell/pages/errors; global loading/offline/localized-error UX implemented in layout and error routes (`+layout.svelte`, `+error.svelte`, API failure normalization); async actions show loading and suppress duplicate submits (`+page.svelte` with `pendingActionKey` + disabled buttons); responsive baseline is role-specific (mobile-first employee, desktop/tablet vendor/admin via `presentation.ts`/`shell.ts`). API access is through the generated TS client in `platform/api/client.ts` with no direct `fetch` usage found in `apps/web/src`. Automated checks executed via `node --import tsx --test tests/**/*.test.ts` in `apps/web` passed (20/20).

reinforcementResults.name: No-Legacy/Fallback Gate
reinforcementResults.summary: No blocking issues found for this feature. The workspace implements a shared SvelteKit app for `/employee/*`, `/vendor/*`, and `/admin/*` with role-aware navigation; zh-TW UI copy is centralized; and global loading/offline/localized-error UX is present. Frontend API access is centralized through the generated TypeScript client layer. Policy evidence checks in scope are satisfied (production runtime wired to SQL persistence, mock-only auth phase remains enforced, optional runtime capabilities remain flag-gated). Runtime verification commands could not be executed in this sandbox due EPERM restrictions (tsx IPC/socket creation and `svelte-kit sync` file writes).

executionConstraints: Generated TS client is sole API access layer.
Maintain mobile-first employee UX; desktop-first vendor/admin UX with tablet stability.
name: Shared Frontend Platform and UX Baseline
rationale: Shared frontend primitives avoid cross-portal rework.
summary: Build common routing, i18n, async-state, and error-handling foundations.
